commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: But in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men. See the annotations Matthew xv. It is groundless to pretend from this text, that the precepts and traditions of the Church are not binding and obligatory, for Christ himself has commanded all to hear his Church, and obey their lawful priests. These indeed may be called the precepts of men, but they are precepts of men invested with power and authority from God, and of whom Christ himself said, (Luke x. ) He that heareth you, heareth me; and he that despiseth you, despiseth me.
